---
title: "Analysis_NES"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2025-03-25"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, lme4, ggplot2, dplyr, lmerTest, arrow, stringi, lubridate, zoo, patchwork)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.
```{r}
setwd("/Users/halfidaldal/Documents/PENPAL_analysis")
df_sentida <- read_parquet("data/processed/dyadic_sentiment_scores.parquet")
df_sentida_humanlike <- read_parquet("data/processed/dyadic_sentiment_scores_simulated.parquet")


```


## Including Plots


You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r}
setwd("/Users/halfidaldal/Documents/PENPAL_analysis")



df_sentida_humanlike <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  rename(
    ai_valence_score = ai_sentiment_score,
    user_valence_score = user_sentiment_score
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    user_token_count = stri_count_words(user),
    ai_token_count = stri_count_words(ai)
  )

df_sentida <- df_sentida %>%
  rename(
    ai_valence_score = ai_sentiment_score,
    user_valence_score = user_sentiment_score
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    user_token_count = stri_count_words(user),
    ai_token_count = stri_count_words(ai)
  ) 

```



```{r}


mean_tokens_user <- mean(df_sentida$user_token_count, na.rm = TRUE)
mean_tokens_ai   <- mean(df_sentida$ai_token_count, na.rm = TRUE)

cat("Mean user token length:", mean_tokens_user, "\n")
cat("Mean AI token length:", mean_tokens_ai, "\n")

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = ai_token_count, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on AI token count",
    x = "AI token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = user_token_count, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on user token count",
    x = "User token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )


df_sentida <- df_sentida %>%
  mutate(token_ratio = if_else(ai_token_count == 0, NA_real_, user_token_count / ai_token_count))  


ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = token_ratio, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "#4DAF4A") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on User/AI Token Ratio",
    x = "Token Ratio (User / AI)",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  ) +
  xlim(0, 1.5)  # optional: cut off extreme outliers


cosine_sim <- function(x, y) {
  if (is.null(x) || is.null(y) || any(is.na(x)) || any(is.na(y))) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  return(sum(x * y) / (sqrt(sum(x^2)) * sqrt(sum(y^2))))
}

df_sentida <- df_sentida %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  arrange(.by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(previous_ai_embedding = lag(ai_embedding)) %>%
  mutate(previous_ai_valence = lag(ai_valence_score)) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(cosine_sim_prev_ai = cosine_sim(user_corrected_embedding, previous_ai_embedding)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = ai_token_count, y = cosine_sim_prev_ai)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim prev_ai based on user token count",
    x = "User token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = user_token_count, y = cosine_sim_prev_ai)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim prev_ai based on user token count",
    x = "User token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = token_ratio, y = cosine_sim_prev_ai)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Cosine Similarity (User vs. Previous AI)",
       x = "User/AI Token Ratio", y = "Cosine Similarity to Previous AI") +
  xlim(0, 1.5) +
  theme_minimal()

# Make both data frames compatible
df_sentida <- df_sentida["cosine_similarity"]
df_sentida$source <- "Field"

df_sentida_humanlike <- df_sentida_humanlike["cosine_similarity"]
df_sentida_humanlike$source <- "Simulated"

df_long <- rbind(df_sentida, df_sentida_humanlike)

# Plot
ggplot(df_long, aes(x = cosine_similarity, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  scale_fill_brewer(
    palette = "Set1",
    name = "Source",
    labels = c("Field", "Simulated")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Cosine Similarity: Field vs Simulated",
    x = "Cosine Similarity",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )
ggsave("distribution_cosine_sim_combined.png", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")


ggplot(df_lag, aes(x = d12, y = d23)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Story Length") +
  labs(
    title = "Field Rubber-Band Lagged/Shifted",
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Middle−Early)"),
    y     = expression(Delta[23]~"(Late−Middle)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)



```


```{r}

df_valence_class_2 <- read_parquet("data/cosine_sim_embeddings_e5_with_sentiment_2.parquet")
df_cosine_sim_both_datasets <- bind_rows(
  df_sentida  %>% select(cosine_similarity) %>% mutate(source = "field"),
  df_sentida_humanlike %>% select(cosine_similarity) %>% mutate(source = "simulated")
)

ggplot(df_cosine_sim_both_datasets, aes(x = cosine_similarity, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5)
```



```{r}
ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = user_valence_score)) +
  geom_density(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot: User Valence Score", x = "User Valence Score", y = "Density")

ggplot(df_sentida, aes(x = ai_valence_score)) +
  geom_density(fill = "#FF7F00", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot: AI Valence Score", x = "AI Valence Score", y = "Density")

df_long_2 <- df_sentida %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
               names_to = "source", values_to = "valence_score")

ggplot(df_long_2, aes(x = valence_score, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("user_valence_score" = "#377EB8", "ai_valence_score" = "#FF7F00"), name = "Source", labels = c("AI", "User")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Valence Scores: User vs AI",
    x = "Valence Score",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("distribution_valence.png", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

```



```{r}
df_sentida <- df_sentida %>%
  filter(!(client_id %in% c("steno_5", "steno_4")))

# Choose one client/workshop
chosen_client <- "steno_3"
chosen_workshop <- "11"

df_sentida %>%
  mutate(time = ymd_hms(timestamp)) %>%
  filter(client_id == chosen_client, workshop_id == chosen_workshop) %>%
  arrange(time) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  mutate(turn_index = row_number()) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
    names_to = "source",
    values_to = "valence"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = turn_index, y = valence, color = source)) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, span = 0.5, size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", labels = c("AI", "User")) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Smoothed Valence Gap for a single story"),
    subtitle = "Human-AI Model",
    x = "Turn Index",
    y = "Valence Score",
    color = "Source"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )
ggsave("valence_smoothed.jpg", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")



df_sentida %>%
  mutate(time = ymd_hms(timestamp)) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  arrange(time, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(turn_index = row_number()) %>%
  filter(n() >= 4) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
               names_to = "source", values_to = "valence") %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id, source) %>%
  mutate(valence_smoothed = rollmean(valence, k = 8, fill = NA, align = "center")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = turn_index, y = valence_smoothed, color = source)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~client_id + workshop_id, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Rolling Average of Valence (k = 8) Human-AI",
    x = "Turn Index",
    y = "Smoothed Valence Score",
    color = "Source"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

valence_alignment_human_ai_mean <- df_sentida %>%
  mutate(valence_gap = abs(user_valence_score - ai_valence_score)) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_valence_gap = mean(valence_gap, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

valence_alignment_human_ai_mean_prev <- df_sentida %>%
  mutate(valence_gap = abs(user_valence_score - previous_ai_valence)) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_valence_gap = mean(valence_gap, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

valence_alignment_human_ai_pearsons <- df_sentida %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_valence = cor(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score, use = "complete.obs"),
    n_turns = n()
  )

ggsave("grid_view.png", dpi = 300, width = 16, height = 7)

```

```{r}

# 1. Prepare df with turn indices
df_part <- df_sentida %>%
  arrange(id, timestamp) %>%         # ensure chronological within each id
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    turn_idx = row_number(),         # turn index per participant
    n_turns  = n()                   # total turns per participant
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(n_turns >= 3)               # need at least 3 turns to form 3 quantiles

# 2. 3-quantile binning per participant
df_part_q <- df_part %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(q3 = ntile(turn_idx, 3)) %>%  # assign each turn to early/middle/late
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(id, q3) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_user = mean(user_valence_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_ai   = mean(ai_valence_score,   na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3. Plot mean user valence across the three stages
ggplot(df_part_q, aes(x = factor(q3), y = mean_user, group = id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  labs(
    title = "3-Quantile Binned Mean User Valence by Participant",
    x     = "Stage (Early / Middle / Late)",
    y     = "Mean User Valence"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# 4. Compute per-participant linear trends (slopes)
slopes <- df_part %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    user_slope = coef(lm(user_valence_score ~ turn_idx, data = cur_data()))[2],
    ai_slope   = coef(lm(ai_valence_score   ~ turn_idx, data = cur_data()))[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 5. Plot distribution of slopes
slopes_long <- slopes %>%
  pivot_longer(c(user_slope, ai_slope), names_to = "role", values_to = "slope")

ggplot(slopes_long, aes(x = role, y = slope)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(
    title = "Participant-Level Valence Trends (Human–AI)",
    x     = "Role",
    y     = "Slope of Valence over Turns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#    and assign a row-index within each story.
fire <- read_csv("data/firestore_data.csv") %>%
  filter(sandbox == FALSE) %>%           # same sandbox filter
  arrange(client_id, workshop_id, timestamp) %>%
  group_by(client_id, workshop_id) %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%     # 1,2,3… within each group
  ungroup() %>%
  select(client_id, workshop_id, row_idx, id)


# 2) Read your AI–AI valence data and give it exactly the same row-index
hai <- read_parquet("data/valence_scores_e5_instruct_humanlike.parquet") %>%
  arrange(client_id, workshop_id) %>%    # preserves the simulation order
  group_by(client_id, workshop_id) %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%     # 1,2,3… matching the raw data
  ungroup()


# 3) Join on (client_id, workshop_id, row_idx) to pull in the real `id`
hai_labeled <- hai %>%
  left_join(fire, by = c("client_id","workshop_id","row_idx")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(id))   # sanity-check: drop any mismatches

# now hai_labeled$id is exactly the participant id for each turn


# ------ then you can re-run your 3-quantile & slope code on hai_labeled ------

# e.g. 3-quantile mean user valence by participant:
df_aa_q <- hai_labeled %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%                    # need ≥3 turns
  arrange(row_idx) %>%
  mutate(stage = ntile(row_idx, 3)) %>%
  group_by(id, stage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_user = mean(user_valence_score, na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_ai   = mean(ai_valence_score,   na.rm=TRUE),
    .groups="drop"
  )

ggplot(df_aa_q, aes(factor(stage), mean_user, group = id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = .3) +
  geom_point(alpha = .3) +
  labs(
    title = "3-Quantile Mean User Valence by Participant (AI–AI)",
    x = "Stage (Early/Mid/Late)",
    y = "Mean User Valence"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# e.g. per-participant slopes
slopes_aa <- hai_labeled %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  filter(n() >= 2) %>%
  summarise(
    user_slope = coef(lm(user_valence_score ~ row_idx, data = cur_data()))[2],
    ai_slope   = coef(lm(ai_valence_score   ~ row_idx, data = cur_data()))[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  )

slopes_long_aa <- slopes_aa %>%
  pivot_longer(c(user_slope, ai_slope), names_to="role", values_to="slope")

ggplot(slopes_long_aa, aes(role, slope)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill="lightgreen", alpha=.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Participant-Level Valence Trends (AI–AI)",
    x = "Role",
    y = "Slope"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


## AI-AI

```{r}

df_sentida_humanlike <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  mutate(
    ai1_token_count = stri_count_words(ai1),
    ai2_token_count = stri_count_words(ai2)
  ) %>%
  filter(workshop_id != "2")

ggplot(df_sentida_humanlike, aes(x = ai1_token_count, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on AI1 token count",
    x = "AI token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )

ggplot(df_sentida_humanlike, aes(x = ai2_token_count, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on AI2 token count",
    x = "AI token count",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  )


df_sentida_humanlike <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  mutate(token_ratio = if_else(ai2_token_count == 0, NA_real_, ai1_token_count / ai2_token_count))  


ggplot(df_sentida_humanlike, aes(x = token_ratio, y = cosine_similarity)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "#4DAF4A") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Cos Sim based on User/AI Token Ratio",
    x = "Token Ratio (User / AI)",
    y = "Cosine Similarity"
  ) +
  xlim(0.5, 1.5)  # 
```


```{r}
ggplot(df_sentida_humanlike, aes(x = user_valence_score)) +
  geom_density(fill = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot: User Valence Score", x = "User Valence Score", y = "Density")

ggplot(df_sentida_humanlike, aes(x = ai_valence_score)) +
  geom_density(fill = "red", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Density Plot: AI Valence Score", x = "AI Valence Score", y = "Density")

df_long <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
               names_to = "source", values_to = "valence_score")

ggplot(df_long, aes(x = valence_score, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", labels = c("AI", "User")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Valence Scores: User vs AI",
    x = "Valence Score",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("distribution_valence_humanlike.png", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

means_df <- df_long %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(mean_val = mean(valence_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

base_plot <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = valence_score, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  geom_vline(data = means_df,
             aes(xintercept = mean_val, color = source),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2", name = "", labels = c("AI", "User")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )
means_df_2 <- df_long_2 %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(mean_val = mean(valence_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

base_plot_2 <- ggplot(df_long_2, aes(x = valence_score, fill = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4, color = NA) +
  geom_vline(data = means_df_2,
             aes(xintercept = mean_val, color = source),
             linetype = "dashed", size = 0.3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("user_valence_score" = "#377EB8", "ai_valence_score" = "#FF7F00"), name = "", labels = c("AI", "User")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("user_valence_score" = "#377EB8", "ai_valence_score" = "#FF7F00")) +  # optional
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 24),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  )
# Create individual plots with just subtitles
p1 <- base_plot + ggtitle("Simulated Data")
p2 <- base_plot_2 + ggtitle("Field Data")

# Combine and annotate with global title and axis labels
combined_plot <- (p2 | p1) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Distribution of Valence Scores: User vs AI",
    caption = NULL,
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 24),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
    )
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Add global x and y labels using patchwork::inset_element if necessary,
# but the most hack-free and clean way is using cowplot::ggdraw() like this:
library(cowplot)

final_plot <- ggdraw() +
  draw_plot(combined_plot, 0, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_label("Valence Score", x = 0.5, y = 0, vjust = -0.1, size = 18) +
  draw_label("Density", x = 0, y = 0.5, angle = 90, vjust = 1.2, size = 18)
final_plot
ggsave("combined_valence_distributions_clean.png", final_plot, dpi = 300, width = 16, height = 7)


```

```{r, echo=FALSE}


# Choose one client/workshop to visualize
chosen_client <- "steno_3"
chosen_workshop <- "12"

df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  filter(client_id == chosen_client, workshop_id == chosen_workshop) %>%
  arrange(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  mutate(turn_index = row_number()) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
    names_to = "source",
    values_to = "valence"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = turn_index, y = valence, color = source)) +
  geom_smooth(se = FALSE, span = 0.5, size = 1.2) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2", labels = c("AI 1", "AI 2")) +
  labs(
    title = paste("Smoothed Valence Gap"),
    subtitle = "AI–AI Model",
    x = "Turn Index",
    y = "Valence Score",
    color = "Source"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )
ggsave("valence_gap_humanlike.png", dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")



df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  arrange(.by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(turn_index = row_number()) %>%
  filter(n() >= 4) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score),
               names_to = "source", values_to = "valence") %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id, source) %>%
  mutate(valence_smoothed = rollmean(valence, k = 8, fill = NA, align = "center")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = turn_index, y = valence_smoothed, color = source)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~client_id + workshop_id, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    title = "Rolling Average of Valence (k = 8) AI-AI",
    x = "Turn Index",
    y = "Smoothed Valence Score",
    color = "Source"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

valence_alignment_ai_ai_mean <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  mutate(valence_gap = abs(user_valence_score - ai_valence_score)) %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_valence_gap = mean(valence_gap, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

valence_alignment_ai_ai_pearsons <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_valence = cor(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score, use = "complete.obs"),
    n_turns = n()
  )

```
## Comparison

```{r}


# ——————————————————————————————————————————————
# 1) Human–AI (prev)
human_prev <- df_sentida %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  # need to have previous_ai_valence already computed
  filter(!is.na(previous_ai_valence)) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_human_prev   = cor(user_valence_score, previous_ai_valence, use="complete.obs"),
    n_prev_human     = n(),
    .groups="drop"
  )

# 2) Human–AI (same)
human_same <- df_sentida %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_human_same   = cor(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score, use="complete.obs"),
    n_same_human     = n(),
    .groups="drop"
  )

# ——————————————————————————————————————————————
# we now do the same on the AI–AI data
# first compute previous_ai_valence in the AI–AI df:
hai <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  arrange(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  mutate(previous_ai_valence = lag(user_valence_score)) %>%
  ungroup()

# 3) AI–AI (prev)
ai_prev <- hai %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  filter(!is.na(previous_ai_valence)) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_ai_prev      = cor(ai_valence_score, previous_ai_valence, use="complete.obs"),
    n_prev_ai        = n(),
    .groups="drop"
  )

# 4) AI–AI (same)
ai_same <- df_sentida_humanlike %>%
  group_by(workshop_id, client_id) %>%
  summarise(
    cor_ai_same      = cor(user_valence_score, ai_valence_score, # or user_valence_score if your columns differ
                          use="complete.obs"),
    n_same_ai        = n(),
    .groups="drop"
  )

# ——————————————————————————————————————————————
# 5) join all four into one table
all_corrs <- human_prev %>%
  inner_join(human_same, by=c("workshop_id","client_id")) %>%
  inner_join(ai_prev,    by=c("workshop_id","client_id")) %>%
  inner_join(ai_same,    by=c("workshop_id","client_id")) %>%
  # keep only stories with ≥3 in every condition
  filter(
    n_prev_human >= 3,
    n_same_human >= 3,
    n_prev_ai    >= 3,
    n_same_ai    >= 3
  )

# 6) pivot to long form for plotting
corrs_long <- all_corrs %>%
  select(workshop_id, client_id,
         cor_human_prev, cor_human_same,
         cor_ai_prev,    cor_ai_same) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = starts_with("cor_"),
    names_to  = "condition",
    values_to = "rho"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    condition = recode(condition,
      cor_human_prev = "AI–>User (prev turn)",
      cor_human_same = "User–>AI (same turn)",
      cor_ai_prev    = "AI->User (prev turn)",
      cor_ai_same    = "User->AI (same turn)"
    )
  )

# 7) draw the 4 boxplots
boxplot <- ggplot(corrs_long, aes(x=condition, y=rho, fill=condition)) +
    scale_x_discrete(limits = c(
    "User–>AI (same turn)",
    "AI–>User (prev turn)",
    "User->AI (same turn)",
    "AI->User (prev turn)"

  )) +
  geom_boxplot(width=0.6, outlier.shape=NA, alpha=0.8) +
  geom_jitter(width=0.15, alpha=0.4, size=1) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette="Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Per-Story Valence Alignment: Field Data vs Simulated Data\n(prev & same-turn)",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = expression(Pearson~rho~"(valence pairs)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size=14) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    axis.text.x        = element_text(angle=10, hjust=0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    plot.title         = element_text(face="bold", hjust=0.5)
  )

boxplot
ggsave("combined_valence_distributions_clean.png", boxplot,  dpi = 900, width = 16, height = 7)



library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(afex)

# 1) Start from your wide table:
#    all_corrs has columns: cor_human_prev, cor_human_same, cor_ai_prev, cor_ai_same

# 2) Pivot longer with a regex that splits your names into Partner & Turn directly:
dat_z <- all_corrs %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols        = starts_with("cor_"),
    names_to    = c("Partner","Turn"),
    names_pattern = "cor_(.*)_(.*)",
    values_to   = "rho"
  ) %>%
  # 3) Fisher’s z-transform
  mutate(
    rho_z   = atanh(rho),
    # 4) Make them proper factors
    Partner = factor(Partner, levels = c("human","ai"),
                     labels = c("Human","Sim")),
    Turn    = factor(Turn, levels = c("prev","same"),
                     labels = c("Prev","Same"))
  )

# Quick check — you should see both Prev & Same:
table(dat_z$Turn)
#    Prev   Same 
#    100    100   ← something like this
# Basic ANOVA (no repeated measures)
anova_model <- aov(rho_z ~ Partner * Turn, data = dat_z)
summary(anova_model)


# filter for each condition and test
tests <- dat_z %>%
  group_by(Partner,Turn) %>%
  summarise(
    # one‐sample t‐test on z transformed r against mu=0
    t     = t.test(rho_z, mu=0)$statistic,
    p     = t.test(rho_z, mu=0)$p.value,
    meanZ = mean(rho_z),
    n     = n()
  )
tests



```


```{r}
ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Partner, y = rho, group = client_id)) +
  # connecting lines
  geom_line(
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
    color    = "grey70",
    alpha    = 0.3
  ) +
  # boxplots
  geom_boxplot(
    aes(fill = Partner),
    width      = 0.6,
    outlier.shape = NA,
    alpha      = 0.8,
    position   = position_dodge(width = 0.6)
  ) +
  # jitter with dodge
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = Partner),
    position = position_jitterdodge(
      dodge.width  = 0.6,
      jitter.width = 0.15
    ),
    size      = 1.5,
    alpha     = 0.6
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun      = mean,
    geom     = "point",
    shape    = 23,
    size     = 3,
    fill     = "white",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Turn, nrow = 1) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Valence Alignment: Field (Human) vs Simulated (AI) by Turn",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = expression(Pearson~rho~"(per story)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x        = element_text(size = 12),
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
  )
```


```{r}


# 0) your existing per‐turn df
df_part_gap <- df_sentida %>%
  arrange(id, ymd_hms(timestamp)) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    turn_idx    = row_number(),
    n_turns     = n(),
    valence_gap = abs(user_valence_score - ai_valence_score),
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(n_turns >= 3)   # need ≥3 turns

# 1) per‐participant turn counts and median cutoff
turn_counts_hh <- df_part_gap %>%
  distinct(id, n_turns) 
med_hh <- median(turn_counts_hh$n_turns)

# 2) 3‐quantile mean_gap with high/low tag
df_part_gap_q2 <- df_part_gap %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(stage = ntile(turn_idx, 3)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(id, stage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gap  = mean(valence_gap, na.rm=TRUE),
    n_turns   = first(n_turns),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    volume = if_else(n_turns >= med_hh, "High ≥ median", "Low < median")
  )

# 3) Plot, colored by volume
ggplot(df_part_gap_q2, aes(factor(stage), mean_gap, group = id, color = volume)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", aes(group = volume),
               size = 1.2, position = position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1"="Early","2"="Middle","3"="Late")) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Valence Gap by Turn Stage (Human–AI)",
    x     = "Turn Stage",
    y     = "Mean |v_user − v_AI|",
    color = "Story Length"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.5))
ggsave("stage_valence_humanlike_lagged_stage_random.png", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

# 1) Pull in the id mapping from firestore_data.csv
fire <- read_csv("data/firestore_data.csv") %>%
  filter(sandbox == FALSE) %>%
  arrange(client_id, workshop_id, timestamp) %>%
  group_by(client_id, workshop_id) %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(client_id, workshop_id, row_idx, id)

# 2) Read AI–AI valence data & align row_idx
hai_labeled <- read_parquet("data/valence_scores_e5_instruct_humanlike.parquet") %>%
  arrange(client_id, workshop_id) %>%
  group_by(client_id, workshop_id) %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(fire, by = c("client_id","workshop_id","row_idx")) %>%
  filter(!is.na(id))

# (re‐label your hai_labeled if needed)
df_aa <- hai_labeled %>%
  mutate(valence_gap = abs(user_valence_score - ai_valence_score)) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    turn_idx = row_number(),
    n_turns  = n()
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(n_turns >= 3)

turn_counts_aa <- df_aa %>%
  distinct(id, n_turns)
med_aa <- median(turn_counts_aa$n_turns)

df_aa_gap_q2 <- df_aa %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(stage = ntile(turn_idx, 3)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(id, stage) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gap = mean(valence_gap, na.rm=TRUE),
    n_turns  = first(n_turns),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    volume = if_else(n_turns >= med_aa, "High ≥ median", "Low < median")
  )

ggplot(df_aa_gap_q2, aes(factor(stage), mean_gap, group = id, color = volume)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", aes(group = volume),
               size = 1.2, position = position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(width=0.2)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1"="Early","2"="Middle","3"="Late")) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Valence Gap by Turn Stage (AI–AI)",
    x     = "Turn Stage",
    y     = "Mean |v₁ − v₂|",
    color = "Story Length"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title=element_text(hjust=0.5))
ggsave("stage_valence_humanlike.png", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

```



```{r}

# build wide2 for Human–AI, including volume:
wide_hh <- df_part_gap_q2 %>%
  filter(stage %in% 1:3) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols   = c(id, volume),
    names_from = stage,
    values_from = mean_gap,
    names_prefix = "g"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(g1) & !is.na(g2) & !is.na(g3)) %>%
  mutate(
    d12 = g2 - g1,
    d23 = g3 - g2
  )

# scatter + regression per‐group:
ggplot(wide_hh, aes(x = d12, y = d23, color = volume)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    aes(linetype = volume),
    se = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Stage2–Stage1)"),
    y     = expression(Delta[23]~"(Stage3–Stage2)"),
    title = "Rubber-band Dynamics by Story Length"
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("stage_valence_lm_unlagged.png", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

# Fit interaction model:
lm_hh_int <- lm(d23 ~ d12 * volume, data = wide_hh)
summary(lm_hh_int)

# assume df_aa_gap_q has columns (id, stage, mean_gap)
# ——— AI–AI Rubber-Band by Story Length ———

# 0) you already have df_aa_gap_q2 with columns (id, stage, mean_gap, n_turns, volume)

# 1) Pivot wide so we get g1,g2,g3 and keep `volume`
wide_aa <- df_aa_gap_q2 %>%
  filter(stage %in% 1:3) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = c(id, volume),
    names_from  = stage,
    values_from = mean_gap,
    names_prefix= "g"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(g1) & !is.na(g2) & !is.na(g3)) %>%
  mutate(
    d12 = g2 - g1,    # change from Early→Middle
    d23 = g3 - g2     # change from Middle→Late
  )

# 2) Scatter + separate regression lines by volume
ggplot(wide_aa, aes(x = d12, y = d23, color = volume)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    aes(linetype = volume),
    method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "AI–AI Valence-Gap Rubber-Band by Story Length",
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Middle−Early)"),
    y     = expression(Delta[23]~"(Late−Middle)"),
    color = "Story Length",
    linetype = "Story Length"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
ggsave("stage_valence_lm_humanlike.png", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

# 3) Fit an interaction model
lm_aa_int <- lm(d23 ~ d12 * volume, data = wide_aa)
summary(lm_aa_int)
```
```{r}
library(dplyr)
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# DROP-IN: Permutation, Global-Shuffle & Lag Tests for Rubber-Band Slope
#
# Assumes you have already created:
#   df_part_gap_q2   # per-id, per-stage summary with columns id, stage, mean_gap, volume
#   df_part_gap      # per-turn data with id, turn_idx, valence_gap
#   wide_hh          # wide version with d12, d23 (you can ignore it here)
#
library(dplyr)

# 0) Observed rubber-band slope across all IDs
obs_slope <- coef(lm(d23 ~ d12, data = wide_hh))[2]
cat("Observed rubber-band slope =", round(obs_slope, 3), "\n\n")

# ─────────────── 1) WITHIN-ID PERMUTATION TEST ───────────────
set.seed(2025)
n_sims <- 1000
perm_slopes <- replicate(n_sims, {
  df_perm <- df_part_gap_q2 %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    mutate(stage_p = sample(stage)) %>%    # shuffle within each id
    ungroup() %>%
    filter(stage_p %in% 1:3) %>%
    pivot_wider(
      id_cols     = id,
      names_from  = stage_p,
      values_from = mean_gap,
      names_prefix= "g",
      values_fn   = mean,      # collapse duplicates if any
      values_fill = NA
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(g1) & !is.na(g2) & !is.na(g3)) %>%
    transmute(
      d12 = g2 - g1,
      d23 = g3 - g2
    )
  coef(lm(d23 ~ d12, data = df_perm))[2]
})

p_perm <- mean(abs(perm_slopes) >= abs(obs_slope))
cat("Permutation test (n =", n_sims, "):\n",
    "  mean(null slopes) =", round(mean(perm_slopes), 3), "\n",
    "  sd(null slopes)   =", round(sd(perm_slopes), 3), "\n",
    "  p-value           =", signif(p_perm, 3), "\n\n", sep="")

# ─────────────── 2) GLOBAL-SHUFFLE TEST ───────────────
set.seed(2025)
df_global <- df_part_gap_q2 %>%
  mutate(stage_g = sample(stage)) %>%      # shuffle across all IDs
  group_by(id, stage_g) %>%
  summarise(mean_gap = mean(mean_gap), .groups = "drop") %>%  # collapse any duplicates
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = id,
    names_from  = stage_g,
    values_from = mean_gap,
    names_prefix= "g",
    values_fn   = mean,
    values_fill = NA
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(g1) & !is.na(g2) & !is.na(g3)) %>%
  transmute(
    d12 = g2 - g1,
    d23 = g3 - g2
  )

slope_global <- coef(lm(d23 ~ d12, data = df_global))[2]
cat("Global-shuffle slope =", round(slope_global, 3), "\n\n")

# ─────────────── 3) LAGGED-STAGE TEST ───────────────
df_lag <- df_part_gap %>%                  # back to per-turn data
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(
    stage_real = ntile(turn_idx, 3),
    stage_lag  = lag(stage_real)
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  filter(!is.na(stage_lag)) %>%
  group_by(id, stage_lag) %>%
  summarise(mean_gap = mean(valence_gap, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = id,
    names_from  = stage_lag,
    values_from = mean_gap,
    names_prefix= "g",
    values_fn   = mean,
    values_fill = NA
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(g1) & !is.na(g2) & !is.na(g3)) %>%
  transmute(
    d12 = g2 - g1,
    d23 = g3 - g2
  )

ggplot(df_lag, aes(x = d12, y = d23)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm", se = FALSE, size = 1, color = "blue"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Field Rubber-Band Lagged/Shifted",
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Middle−Early)"),
    y     = expression(Delta[23]~"(Late−Middle)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("Field_rubber_band_lagged.png", path = "graphs", dpi = 900,
       width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")
ggsave("Field_rubber_band_lagged.png", path = "graphs", dpi = 900, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

slope_lag <- coef(lm(d23 ~ d12, data = df_lag))[2]
cat("Lagged-stage slope  =", round(slope_lag, 3), "\n")
# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

```{r}
flips <- wide2 %>%
  mutate(
    sign12 = sign(d12),   # +1 if gap rose, −1 if fell
    sign23 = sign(d23),
    reversal = sign12 * sign23 < 0  # TRUE when they differ
  )

# what proportion of participants “flip”?
flip_rate <- mean(flips$reversal, na.rm=TRUE)
flip_rate
# test against 50% null using a binomial
binom.test(sum(flips$reversal), nrow(flips), p=0.5, alternative="greater")

library(dplyr)

# one row per participant with their total #turns and mean valence
df_participant_valence <- df_sentida %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    n_turns    = n(),
    mean_user  = mean(user_valence_score, na.rm=TRUE),
    mean_ai    = mean(ai_valence_score,   na.rm=TRUE)
  ) %>%
  filter(n_turns >= 3)

# scatter + loess
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df_participant_valence, aes(x = n_turns, y = mean_user)) +
  geom_point(alpha=.5) +
  geom_smooth(method="lm", se=TRUE) +
  labs(title="Mean User Valence vs. #Turns",
       x="Number of Turns", y="Mean User Valence") +
  theme_minimal()

# Pearson
with(df_participant_valence, cor.test(n_turns, mean_user))

```
```{r}
wide2 <- wide %>%
  mutate(
    d12 = g2 - g1,
    d23 = g3 - g2,
    abs_d12 = abs(d12),
    abs_d23 = abs(d23),
    # total two‐step oscillation:
    amp2 = abs_d12 + abs_d23,
    # or net displacement from start to end:
    amp1 = abs(g3 - g1)
  )

# boxplots of total swing size:
ggplot(wide2, aes(x = condition, y = amp2)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title="Total Valence‐Gap Oscillation (Stage1→2 + Stage2→3)",
       x="Human–AI vs AI–AI", y="Amplitude (abs_d12 + abs_d23)")

# or test statistically:
t.test(amp2 ~ condition, data=wide2)
```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(cowplot)

# HUMAN–AI plot
p_hai <- ggplot(df_part_gap_q2, aes(factor(stage), mean_gap, group = id, color = volume)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", aes(group = volume),
               size = 1.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Early", "2" = "Middle", "3" = "Late")) +
  labs(
    title = "Field Data",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Story Length"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# AI–AI plot
p_aiai <- ggplot(df_aa_gap_q2, aes(factor(stage), mean_gap, group = id, color = volume)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", aes(group = volume),
               size = 1.2, position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point",
               size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.2)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("1" = "Early", "2" = "Middle", "3" = "Late")) +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated Data",
    x     = NULL,
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Story Length"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Combine with patchwork and shared main title
combo <- (p_hai | p_aiai) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Mean Valence Gap by Turn Stage",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 24, face = "bold")
    )
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Add global x and y labels
final <- ggdraw() +
  draw_plot(combo, 0, 0, 1, 1) +
  draw_label("Mean Valence Difference", x = 0.007, y = 0.2, angle = 90, hjust = 0, size = 18) +
  draw_label("Turn Stage", x = 0.5, y = 0.02, vjust = 0, size = 18)

# Save if needed
# ggsave("valence_gap_turnstage.png", final, dpi = 300, width = 14, height = 6)
final
# Save
ggsave("combined_stage_valence_gap.png", final, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)

```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# Plot: Human–AI
p_lm_hh <- ggplot(wide_hh, aes(x = d12, y = d23, color = volume)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(linetype = volume), se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Field Data",
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Middle − Early)"),
    y     = expression(Delta[23]~"(Late − Middle)"),
    color = "Story Length",
    linetype = "Story Length"
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text      = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.y   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    legend.title   = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text    = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Plot: AI–AI
p_lm_aa <- ggplot(wide_aa, aes(x = d12, y = d23, color = volume)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, lty = 2, color = "grey60") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", aes(linetype = volume), se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Simulated Data",
    x     = expression(Delta[12]~"(Middle − Early)"),
    y     = NULL,
    color = "Story Length",
    linetype = "Story Length"
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title     = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text      = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.title.x   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title.y   = element_blank(),
    legend.title   = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text    = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

# Combine with patchwork and shared title
rubber_plot <- p_lm_hh + p_lm_aa +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Rubber-Band Dynamics in Valence Gap: Field vs Simulated Data",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 24)
    )
  ) &
  theme(legend.position = "top")

# Save the plot
ggsave("combined_rubber_band_dynamics.png", rubber_plot, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
# 2. Valence distribution summaries
valence_stats <- bind_rows(
  df_long_2 %>% mutate(source_type = "Field"),
  df_long   %>% mutate(source_type = "Simulated")
) %>%
  group_by(source_type, source) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_valence = mean(valence_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_valence   = sd(valence_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    var_valence  = var(valence_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

valence_stats
```
